title: Fixture: Silver-tier low-criticality UC for tests
spl:
index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd log_level=WARN component=ConfReplication earliest=-24h
| stats count AS warn_count BY host
| where warn_count > 5
description: Lists search heads or indexers logging more than five configuration-replication warnings in twenty-four hours.
value: Elevated ConfReplication warnings often precede bundle push failures or cluster desync. Low-criticality tracking gives platform engineers a backlog item before users see missing knowledge objects.
grandmaExplanation: We notice when Splunk servers complain many times about syncing their settings. That helps the platform team fix small problems before dashboards go missing.
implementation: Schedule daily search, post results to #splunk-platform Slack channel, no paging.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Role can search index=_internal on all cluster members.

Step 1 — Confirm data
`| search index=_internal component=ConfReplication | head 5` should return recent events on each member.

Step 2 — Create the search
Daily cron at 08:00 local; threshold five warnings is informational.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare warn_count to splunkd.log grep on a sample host.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Track trend weekly; escalate manually if count doubles week-over-week.
